workspace.name: Meridian Software — Finance Operations
workspace.description: Synthetic demo workspace modeled on a mid-market B2B SaaS finance org. All data is fictional.

processes.name: AP Invoice Exception Handling
processes.objective: Resolve vendor invoices that fail 3-way match (PO, receipt, invoice) so they can post to NetSuite and enter the payment run.

current_version.change_summary: Added Coupa comment requirement before rejecting an invoice back to the vendor.

steps.name: Pull exception queue
steps.description: Open the NetSuite saved search 'AP – Failed 3-Way Match' each morning.

steps.name: Classify the exception
steps.description: Determine root cause: price variance, quantity variance, missing PO, or missing receipt.

steps.name: Locate the PO
steps.description: Find the referenced PO in Coupa. If the vendor omitted the PO number, search vendor email threads in Outlook.

steps.name: Correct and re-match
steps.description: Fix the mismatch (update PO line, request receipt, or edit invoice header) and re-run the match.

steps.name: Comment and route
steps.description: Log resolution in Coupa comments; route price variances above $2,500 to the procurement lead for approval.

steps.name: Post to NetSuite
steps.description: Approved invoices post automatically on the next sync.

policies: Price variances >$2,500 require procurement lead approval.
Never bypass a failed match by keying a manual journal entry.
All rejections back to vendors must include a Coupa comment (added in v7).

history.change_summary: Added Coupa comment requirement before rejecting an invoice back to the vendor.
Documented the Outlook search fallback for missing PO numbers.

processes.name: Revenue Recognition
processes.objective: Recognize subscription and services revenue under ASC 606 each month, producing the rev-rec journal entries and deferred revenue waterfall.

current_version.change_summary: Removed the second-reviewer sign-off on the deferred revenue waterfall; ARM reconciliation report now considered sufficient.

steps.name: Sync closed-won contracts
steps.description: NetSuite ARM ingests closed-won opportunities and amendments from Salesforce CPQ nightly.

steps.name: Review new revenue arrangements
steps.description: Revenue accountant reviews new arrangements for non-standard terms (usage tiers, ramp deals, multi-element).

steps.name: Run rev-rec schedules
steps.description: ARM generates recognition schedules and proposed journal entries.

steps.name: Reconcile waterfall
steps.description: Tie the deferred revenue waterfall to the GL; investigate variances >$10k.

steps.name: Post journal entries
steps.description: Controller approves and posts the rev-rec batch (control R2R-04).

policies: All non-standard deals require a documented ASC 606 memo (control R2R-07).
Rev-rec batch posting requires controller approval (control R2R-04).
Waterfall variances >$10k must be resolved before close day 5.

history.change_summary: Removed the second-reviewer sign-off on the deferred revenue waterfall; ARM reconciliation report now considered sufficient.
Added usage-tier true-up substep to arrangement review.
Migrated waterfall reconciliation from Excel to the ARM reconciliation report.

processes.name: Vendor Onboarding (US)
processes.objective: Collect W-9, banking details, and compliance attestations for new US vendors and create the vendor record in NetSuite and Coupa.

current_version.change_summary: Added OFAC screening step before record creation.

steps.name: Send intake form
steps.description: Email the vendor the onboarding packet (W-9, ACH form, security questionnaire).

steps.name: Chase and collect documents
steps.description: Follow up by email until all documents are returned; verify W-9 TIN against IRS records.

steps.name: OFAC screening
steps.description: Screen the vendor against the OFAC SDN list; save the screenshot to the vendor folder.

steps.name: Verify banking by callback
steps.description: Call the vendor at a number sourced independently of the email thread to confirm ACH details.

steps.name: Create vendor records
steps.description: Key the vendor into Coupa; the NetSuite record syncs from Coupa.

policies: Banking details must be verified by callback before any payment.
No vendor record without completed OFAC screening.

history.change_summary: Added OFAC screening step before record creation.
Added independent-callback banking verification after a fraud attempt.

processes.name: Vendor Onboarding (EMEA)
processes.objective: Collect tax forms, banking details (IBAN), and compliance attestations for new EMEA vendors and create the vendor record in NetSuite.

current_version.change_summary: Documented VAT number validation via VIES.

steps.name: Send intake email
steps.description: Email the vendor a request for tax residency form, IBAN, and VAT number.

steps.name: Validate VAT via VIES
steps.description: Check the VAT number on the EU VIES portal; save a PDF of the result.

steps.name: Verify banking by callback
steps.description: Call the vendor to confirm IBAN details.

steps.name: Create vendor record
steps.description: Key the vendor directly into NetSuite (EMEA does not use Coupa).

policies: Banking details must be verified by callback before any payment.

history.change_summary: Documented VAT number validation via VIES.

processes.name: Month-End Close Checklist
processes.objective: Execute the month-end close on a 5-business-day timeline, coordinating subledger closes, reconciliations, and reporting sign-offs in FloQast.

current_version.change_summary: Moved AP subledger close from day 2 to day 1 to relieve day-3 bottleneck.

steps.name: Close AP subledger
steps.description: AP resolves or accrues open exceptions and closes the subledger (day 1).

steps.name: Close AR and billing
steps.description: Billing finalizes invoicing; AR posts cash receipts (day 2).

steps.name: Run revenue recognition
steps.description: Controllership executes the rev-rec process and posts the batch (day 3).

steps.name: Balance sheet reconciliations
steps.description: Preparers complete recons in FloQast; reviewers sign off (day 4).

steps.name: Flux analysis and reporting
steps.description: Run flux analysis, draft the reporting package, controller sign-off (day 5).

policies: No close task may be signed off by its preparer.
Close calendar is published in FloQast before day 1.

history.change_summary: Moved AP subledger close from day 2 to day 1 to relieve day-3 bottleneck.
Added pre-close accrual review meeting on business day -2.

processes.name: Quote Approval
processes.objective: Approve non-standard sales quotes (discounts, payment terms, legal redlines) before order form signature.

steps.name: Submit quote
steps.description: Rep submits quote in CPQ; approval matrix routes by discount tier and term changes.

steps.name: Deal desk review
steps.description: Deal desk validates pricing, terms, and rev-rec impact for non-standard elements.

steps.name: Finance/legal escalation
steps.description: Deals with >30% discount or redlined terms route to finance and legal.

policies: Non-standard payment terms require controller review for rev-rec impact.

processes.name: Sales Order Entry
processes.objective: Convert signed order forms into NetSuite sales orders with correct billing schedules and rev-rec treatment.

current_version.change_summary: Documented the manual re-entry path for CPQ sync failures.

steps.name: CPQ-to-NetSuite sync
steps.description: Signed order forms sync from Salesforce CPQ to NetSuite as sales orders.

steps.name: Exception re-entry
steps.description: Orders that fail sync validation are keyed manually into NetSuite from the order form PDF.

steps.name: Billing schedule review
steps.description: Rev ops verifies billing schedule and provisioning trigger for each order.

policies: Manually re-keyed orders require a second-person review before invoicing.

history.change_summary: Documented the manual re-entry path for CPQ sync failures.

processes.name: Invoice Generation
processes.objective: Generate and deliver customer invoices from NetSuite billing schedules, including consolidated and portal-submitted invoices.

current_version.change_summary: Added Ariba portal submission substep for enterprise accounts.

steps.name: Run billing batch
steps.description: NetSuite generates invoices from billing schedules on the 1st.

steps.name: Portal submissions
steps.description: For ~60 enterprise accounts, manually upload invoices to customer AP portals (Ariba, Coupa, Tungsten).

steps.name: Delivery confirmation
steps.description: Confirm portal acceptance; re-submit rejects with corrected PO or entity fields.

policies: Portal rejects must be resolved within 3 business days.

history.change_summary: Added Ariba portal submission substep for enterprise accounts.

processes.name: Collections & Dunning
processes.objective: Collect overdue receivables via automated dunning sequences and escalation to collectors, keeping DSO under 52 days.

current_version.change_summary: Added CSM notification before final dunning notice.

steps.name: Automated dunning
steps.description: Tesorio sends the 15/30/45-day reminder sequence.

steps.name: Collector outreach
steps.description: Accounts >60 days past due are assigned to a collector for direct outreach.

steps.name: Escalation
steps.description: Notify the CSM, then send final notice; >90 days goes to the controller for hold/write-off decision.

policies: Service holds require controller approval.

history.change_summary: Added CSM notification before final dunning notice.

processes.name: Purchase Requisition
processes.objective: Route purchase requests through budget-owner and procurement approval, producing a PO in Coupa.

current_version.change_summary: Security review gate added for new SaaS purchases.

steps.name: Submit requisition
steps.description: Requester submits in Coupa; routing by amount and category.

steps.name: Budget owner approval
steps.description: Cost-center owner approves against budget.

steps.name: Procurement review
steps.description: Procurement checks vendor status and negotiates if >$25k; new SaaS triggers security review.

steps.name: PO issued
steps.description: Coupa issues the PO to the vendor.

policies: No PO, no pay — invoices without a PO reference are rejected.

history.change_summary: Security review gate added for new SaaS purchases.

processes.name: Weekly Payment Run
processes.objective: Execute the weekly ACH/wire payment batch for approved invoices, with dual authorization.

current_version.change_summary: Dual-authorization threshold lowered to $10k for wires.

steps.name: Build payment batch
steps.description: AP selects approved invoices due within 7 days in NetSuite.

steps.name: First approval
steps.description: AP manager reviews the batch against the cash forecast.

steps.name: Release in bank portal
steps.description: Treasury releases ACH batch; wires >$10k need a second authorizer (control P2P-09).

policies: Wires >$10k require dual authorization (control P2P-09).
No same-day vendor banking changes and payment release.

history.change_summary: Dual-authorization threshold lowered to $10k for wires.

processes.name: Balance Sheet Reconciliation
processes.objective: Prepare and review balance sheet account reconciliations in FloQast by close day 4.

steps.name: Auto-certify eligible accounts
steps.description: FloQast auto-certifies zero-balance and threshold-eligible accounts.

steps.name: Prepare reconciliations
steps.description: Preparers tie GL balances to subledgers/bank statements and attach support.

steps.name: Review and sign off
steps.description: Reviewers sign off in FloQast (control R2R-11); items >$25k unreconciled escalate to controller.

policies: Preparer and reviewer must be different people (control R2R-11).

processes.name: Flux Analysis
processes.objective: Explain month-over-month and budget-vs-actual variances above materiality thresholds for the reporting package.

current_version.change_summary: Initial documented version from capture sessions.

steps.name: Export TB and build flux file
steps.description: Export trial balance from NetSuite, refresh the Excel flux model.

steps.name: Draft variance explanations
steps.description: FP&A drafts explanations for variances over threshold, chasing detail with process owners over Slack.

steps.name: Controller review
steps.description: Controller challenges explanations and approves the final flux commentary.

policies: Every variance >$50k or 10% requires a written explanation.

history.change_summary: Initial documented version from capture sessions.

observations.rationale: Routine morning exception-queue session covering 14 failed matches.
negative_deviations: PO number missing on 5 of 14 invoices; analyst searched Outlook threads and re-keyed PO numbers into NetSuite by hand.
NetSuite match re-run failed twice on the same invoice; analyst retried until the sync job picked it up.
positive_deviations: Analyst kept a personal Excel tracker of recurring vendors with missing POs — not part of the documented process but visibly speeds up classification.
observations.narrative: Analyst opened the 'AP – Failed 3-Way Match' saved search (14 items). For 5 invoices from recurring vendors (Northwind Facilities, KJ Staffing), the PO number was absent from the invoice header. The analyst searched Outlook for the vendor thread, found the PO in an email footer, and re-keyed it into the NetSuite invoice record. One invoice required two match re-runs before the Coupa sync registered the fix. Two price variances >$2,500 were routed to procurement per policy.

negative_deviations: Missing PO numbers on 4 of 11 invoices — same Outlook-search-and-re-key workaround as prior sessions.
Coupa comment step skipped on one rejection (new v7 requirement not yet habitual).
observations.narrative: Same morning pattern. 4 of 11 invoices lacked PO references, all from vendors onboarded before the 'no PO no pay' policy. Analyst re-keyed POs from email into NetSuite. One rejection to vendor went out without the newly required Coupa comment.

observations.rationale: Covering analyst ran the queue; 16 failed matches.
negative_deviations: Missing PO numbers on 6 of 16 invoices; covering analyst was unaware of the Outlook fallback and pinged the AP channel in Slack, waiting ~40 minutes for guidance before re-keying POs manually.
observations.narrative: Covering analyst hit the same missing-PO pattern but lacked the tribal knowledge of the Outlook search fallback. Waited on Slack for guidance, then manually re-keyed 6 PO numbers. Session ran ~35 minutes over the typical window.

observations.rationale: Month-end heavy queue, 22 failed matches (day-1 close pressure).
negative_deviations: Missing PO numbers on 7 of 22 invoices — Outlook search and manual re-key again.
Under close pressure, two low-dollar exceptions were accrued rather than resolved, deferring work into next month.
positive_deviations: Analyst batched all procurement-approval items into a single Slack thread with a deadline, cutting approval wait time versus one-off pings.
observations.narrative: Month-end queue spike. The missing-PO re-key pattern consumed roughly 45 minutes of the session. To make the day-1 AP subledger close (new v9 close calendar), the analyst accrued two unresolved low-dollar exceptions.

negative_deviations: Missing PO numbers on 4 of 12 invoices; Outlook search and manual re-key into NetSuite.
observations.narrative: Baseline session under v6. Identical missing-PO workaround observed — this pattern predates the v7 change.

observations.rationale: June close rev-rec run — first execution under v12 (single-reviewer waterfall).
negative_deviations: Waterfall variance of $18.4k on a ramp deal took ~2 hours to trace; under v11 the second reviewer had historically caught these at review. No second set of eyes under v12.
Two usage-tier true-ups were computed in a side Excel file because ARM does not model the customer's tier structure.
positive_deviations: Accountant annotated the ARM reconciliation report with links to each ASC 606 memo, making the audit trail easier to follow.
observations.narrative: First close on v12. The removed second-reviewer sign-off (control-relevant change to R2R-04 adjacency) shifted variance detection to the preparer alone. An $18.4k waterfall variance on a ramp deal was traced to a mis-dated amendment; resolved before day-5 but consumed the evening. Usage-tier true-ups still live outside ARM in Excel.

observations.rationale: May close rev-rec run under v11.
negative_deviations: Usage-tier true-ups computed in a side Excel file outside ARM (same as prior months).
observations.narrative: Standard v11 run. Second reviewer flagged one schedule mis-mapping during waterfall sign-off; corrected same day. Usage-tier Excel workaround present.

negative_deviations: Document chase required 4 follow-up emails for one vendor; no automated reminder exists.
OFAC screenshot saved to a personal folder instead of the vendor folder for one vendor.
observations.narrative: Three vendors onboarded. The intake packet round-trip remains the bottleneck (median 5 days). Callback verification completed for all three.

observations.rationale: June close, day 1–2 coordination under the new v9 calendar.
negative_deviations: AP made the new day-1 subledger close only by accruing unresolved exceptions (see AP exception observations) — the bottleneck moved rather than disappeared.
Two reconciliation preparers were blocked on day 4 waiting for the rev-rec batch that posted late on day 3.
positive_deviations: Pre-close accrual review meeting (added v8) caught three missing accruals before day 1.
observations.narrative: First close on the v9 calendar. Day-1 AP close achieved nominally, but downstream evidence shows exception work was deferred via accruals. Rev-rec posted late day 3 due to the v12 single-reviewer variance chase, compressing day-4 recon work.

negative_deviations: 9 orders failed CPQ→NetSuite sync on entity-mapping errors; each re-keyed manually from the order form PDF (~15 min each).
Second-person review of re-keyed orders happened next day, delaying invoicing for 3 orders.
observations.narrative: Sync failures concentrate on multi-entity deals. Manual re-entry from PDFs is error-prone and the second-person review adds a day of latency.

obs_apex_001.action: Opened saved search 'AP – Failed 3-Way Match' (14 results).
Looked up PO for Northwind Facilities invoice #NF-2214 — no PO reference on invoice.
Searched 'Northwind PO'; found PO-8842 in an email footer from 2026-05-30.
Manually keyed PO-8842 into invoice header; re-ran match — failed (sync lag).
Re-ran match a second time — passed.
Updated personal tracker 'vendors-missing-PO.xlsx' (not in documented process).
Repeated PO search for KJ Staffing invoice #KJ-0067; keyed PO-8911 manually.
Routed two price variances >$2,500 to procurement lead with comments.
Final queue state: 11 resolved, 2 with procurement, 1 pending vendor.

obs_apex_003.action: Posted in #ap-team asking how to handle invoices with no PO number.
Received guidance: search vendor email threads in Outlook for the PO.
Searched threads for 6 vendors; located 6 PO numbers.
Manually keyed 6 PO numbers; re-ran matches.
Final queue state: 12 resolved, 1 with procurement, 3 pending.

obs_revrec_001.action: Ran recognition schedules for June; generated proposed JE batch.
Waterfall reconciliation report showed $18.4k variance vs GL.
Traced variance candidates across ramp-deal amendments.
Identified mis-dated amendment on contract C-11482; corrected schedule start date.
Computed two usage-tier true-ups in side workbook (outside ARM).
Controller approved and posted rev-rec batch (control R2R-04).
Annotated reconciliation report with links to ASC 606 memos.

artifacts.name: Revenue Recognition SOP (ASC 606) — v4
content: Revenue Recognition SOP — Meridian Software (ASC 606)
Version 4 — approved 2026-05-30 by D. Okafor, Controller
1. Scope
This SOP covers monthly revenue recognition for subscription, usage, and professional services revenue.
2. Controls
Control R2R-04: The monthly rev-rec journal batch must be approved by the Controller before posting.
Control R2R-07: Every non-standard arrangement requires a documented ASC 606 memo filed before recognition.
3. Procedure
3.1 ARM ingests closed-won contracts nightly from Salesforce CPQ.
3.2 The revenue accountant reviews all new arrangements flagged non-standard.
3.3 Recognition schedules are generated in ARM; manual schedules are prohibited.
3.4 The deferred revenue waterfall must tie to the GL within $10,000.
3.5 The waterfall reconciliation is reviewed and signed off before batch posting.
NOTE (v4): sign-off is by preparer plus the ARM reconciliation report. Prior versions required a second reviewer.
3.6 Usage-tier true-ups: compute per contract tier definitions. ARM does not currently model tier structures;
an approved Excel template (WB-TRUEUP-02) is permitted until ARM support ships.
4. Exceptions
Any deviation from this SOP must be documented in the close binder and reported to the Controller.

artifacts.name: AP Exception Handling SOP
content: AP Invoice Exception Handling — Standard Operating Procedure
Owner: Priya Raman, AP Manager. Last updated 2026-06-18 (v7).
Daily cadence: work the 'AP – Failed 3-Way Match' queue every business morning.
Root causes seen in practice: price variance, quantity variance, missing PO reference, missing receipt.
Missing PO reference:
If the vendor invoice omits the PO number, locate the PO in Coupa by vendor and amount.
If not found, search the vendor's email thread in Outlook — POs are often quoted in email footers.
Re-key the PO number into the NetSuite invoice header and re-run the match.
Known gap: recurring vendors onboarded before the 'no PO, no pay' policy frequently omit PO numbers.
Escalation: price variances above $2,500 route to the procurement lead.
Rejections: every rejection back to a vendor requires a Coupa comment stating the reason (v7).
Never bypass a failed match with a manual journal entry.

artifacts.name: AP exception queue walkthrough (screen recording)
content: [video artifact — see transcript child artifact art_ap_walkthrough_transcript]

artifacts.name: AP exception queue walkthrough — transcript
content: Transcript excerpt — AP exception walkthrough, 2026-06-24.
"Okay so this one from Northwind again — no PO on the invoice, as usual."
"What I do is search Outlook for the vendor name, the PO is almost always in their email footer."
"Then I paste it into the header field here in NetSuite and re-run the match."
"Sometimes the match fails the first time because Coupa hasn't synced, you just run it again."
"I keep a spreadsheet of the vendors that always do this — it's like the same eight vendors."
"Honestly if the intake portal just required a PO field for these vendors we'd save an hour a day."

content: US Vendor Onboarding Checklist v4
[ ] Intake packet sent (W-9, ACH form, security questionnaire)
[ ] All documents returned and TIN verified
[ ] OFAC SDN screening complete — screenshot saved to vendor folder
[ ] Banking verified by independent callback
[ ] Vendor created in Coupa (NetSuite syncs automatically)

Day -2: pre-close accrual review meeting
